theme_and_archetype.archetype: Archetype 5 (Jewel & Luxury) with elements of Archetype 4 (Swiss & High-Contrast)
theme_and_archetype.description: A premium industrial technology aesthetic, inspired by high-end presentations (Apple/Tesla). It merges deep graphite/charcoal textures with glowing emerald and cyan-green LED accents. It prioritizes realistic materials (brushed metal, glass) and fluid, cinematic scroll storytelling over exaggerated sci-fi tropes.

headings.usage: Use for primary section titles and elegant, cinematic text layers. Tracking should be slightly tight for large scales.

body.usage: Clean, highly readable geometric sans for paragraphs and UI copy.

technical_indicators.usage: Use for digital indicators, timestamps, HUD overlays, and locker numbers. Monospaced.

layout_and_spacing.spacing_scale: Tailwind's generous spacing (e.g., p-12, p-24, gap-16) to create breathing room between cinematic transitions.
layout_and_spacing.locker_grid_composition: A massive CSS Grid of lockers (e.g., 5x4 layout) set inside a wrapper with `perspective: 2000px`. Each locker item acts as a structural portal.
layout_and_spacing.depth_and_perspective: Use CSS 3D transforms (`translateZ`, `rotateX`, `rotateY`). As the user scrolls, the parent wrapper scales and translates in 3D space (`translateZ(1500px)`) to simulate the camera flying through a specific locker door.
layout_and_spacing.mobile_fallback: For screens < 768px, disable the heavy 3D fly-through. Fall back to a refined vertical scroll where each locker 'door' opens as an accordion or simple slide-in to reveal its section. Maintain the metallic texture and LED aesthetic.

motion_and_interactions.scroll_choreography: Bind scroll position to CSS transforms using `framer-motion` (`useScroll`, `useTransform`).
motion_and_interactions.easing: Use smooth, slow cinematic easing. E.g., `cubic-bezier(0.25, 0.1, 0.25, 1)` or `spring` with high damping and low stiffness.
motion_and_interactions.locker_door: The target locker door should animate open (`rotateY(-110deg)`) using `transform-origin: left` to reveal the next section of the site hidden inside.
motion_and_interactions.ambient_animations: Keyframes for breathing LEDs (`opacity: 0.5` to `1`), subtle shifting light glints on the metallic surfaces (via moving linear-gradients), and blinking digital availability indicators.

components_strategy.structure: Custom HTML/Tailwind and framer-motion for the massive 3D scroll scene and locker portals.
components_strategy.ui_overlays: Floating HUD elements (fixed on screen) using Glassmorphism (`backdrop-blur-xl`, `bg-black/40`, `border-white/10`).
components_strategy.forms_and_data: Use Shadcn UI for the Booking Request flow, Newsletter signup, and the interactive Locker Availability Map. Customize Shadcn borders to be `border-white/10` and inputs to be `bg-white/5`.

images.description: Premium futuristic smart locker wall with dark brushed metal and emerald LED glow. Use as the massive structural background or texture for the 3D CSS grid.
Graphite interior of an open locker with mint LED lighting. Use as the background revealed when a locker door flies open.
Cinematic night photography of a modern smart city. Use in the 'Smart City Integration' section.
Sleek smartphone displaying dark UI. Use in the 'Mobile App' section.
Abstract view of a modern industrial setting with teal lighting and metal structures. Great for the 'How it Works' or 'Security' sections.

instructions_to_main_agent: Do NOT use Three.js. The entire scene must be built with standard HTML/CSS and Framer Motion for scroll mapping.
Build a main wrapper with `style={{ perspective: '1200px' }}`.
Inside it, a `motion.div` representing the 'camera/world' which receives `translateZ`, `translateX`, and `translateY` transforms mapped to the window scroll.
Create a grid of 'Locker' components. As scroll progresses, the camera scales/zooms into one specific locker.
The target locker has a 'door' element that swings open on scroll (`rotateY(-110deg)`).
Once the camera moves 'inside' the locker, the opacity of the subsequent section's content fades in.
Implement floating HUDs (e.g., 'SYSTEM ONLINE', 'CAPACITY: 84%') in the corners of the screen using fixed positioning and `JetBrains Mono`.
Add test IDs (`data-testid`) to all interactive elements.
